items.title: Alienware’s Aurora 16X 240Hz RTX 5070 gaming laptop loaded with 64GB RAM/4TB SSD is a whopping $2,500 off today
items.summary: Dell is now offering one of the more loaded configs of the Alienware Aurora 16X gaming laptop for $2,479.99 shipped. This is down from a rather steep $4,980 listed price, putting today’s discount at just over $2,500, or roughly 50% off. We have seen this model listed for less in the past, but it appears to have jumped up due to the rising cost of memory and other components. Even so, $2,480 is a great price for such a packed notebook, particularly with its 64GB of RAM and massive 4TB RAID 0 SSD setup. Head below for more details.

items.title: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation
items.summary: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

items.title: Boston Dynamics tries using ‘robot dogs’ for deliveries
items.summary: Boston Dynamics' robotic quadruped Spot has already found work doing routine factory inspections and patrolling the ruins of Pompeii, but what about deliveries?. items.title: Sony delays its new PS5 fight stick
items.summary: Sony is delaying the launch of its FlexStrike fight stick controller for PS5 and PC, which had been set to launch on August 6th, to an unspecified date in the future. items.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer

items.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
items.summary: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

items.summary: The Iranian government exploited well-known flaws in cellphone networks to locate and then strike U.S. military personnel in the build-up and beginning of the war.
